steps.title: Identify what 'contrast' requires
steps.content: To contrast the two moons' orbits means to show how they differ. The notes provide one key difference: orbital period. Oberon takes 13.46 days per orbit; Miranda takes 1.41 days.

steps.title: Choose the answer that accomplishes the goal
steps.content: Choice A directly contrasts the moons by highlighting that Miranda orbits “many times faster than” Oberon. The other three either generalize about laws without comparing the moons (B, D) or invent orbital shapes the notes don't specify (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
